user_outcome: A SwornAgent TUI operator can launch `sworn`, select a release that exists only on a non-HEAD Git ref, and open its complete board with the catalog's deterministic source ref for plan topology and the farthest advanced valid matching slice status, including uncommitted current-checkout progress.
rationale: The TUI currently has two filesystem-only boundaries: its release list glob and BoardView.LoadBoard's board/status reads. S01 owns catalog discovery and C-01; this slice makes the TUI consume the catalog end to end. The selected ref remains authoritative for plan topology, but source identity is not a freshness proxy for a current checkout's matching status: a bounded, lifecycle-first comparison must surface genuine live progress without letting arbitrary sibling worktrees or stale failures mislead an operator.
in_scope: Replace the Git-repository TUI release-list discovery path with the S01 board catalog. Each ReleaseInfo retained by the list must carry the selected source ref needed to open the same plan; list ordering remains bytewise lexical by release ID and existing keyboard navigation, labels, truncation, and loading feedback remain intact.

When the user presses Enter on a catalog release, propagate that exact source ref through the existing asynchronous board-load command and its stale-result guard. A board-loaded result for the same release name but a different source ref must not overwrite the currently selected board.

Load a selected board plan and its slice IDs through the Git-ref oracle using the catalog-selected source ref instead of `board.ReadBoard`, `os.ReadDir`, or status paths rooted in the primary checkout. Ref-only boards must therefore render tracks and slices even when `docs/release/<release>` does not exist in the checked-out filesystem.

Use the committed Oracle result, resolved owner-track branch before selected release source ref, as the per-slice baseline. Independently read the current checkout's `docs/release/<release>/<slice>/status.json` whether or not the catalog `sourceRef` equals the active branch. Only a readable, parseable file whose `slice_id`, `release`, and `track` exactly match the displayed slice may participate; a missing, malformed, unknown-state, or identity-mismatched live file must retain the committed baseline and must never redirect plan loading or trigger discovery of another ref/worktree.

Elect the displayed state from those two candidates using this complete rule: normal lifecycle rank is `planned` 0, `design_review` 1, `in_progress` 2, `implemented` 3, `verified` 5, and `shipped` 6; `blocked`, `failed_verification`, and `deferred`, plus a `verification.result` of `blocked`, are attention rank 4. The higher normal rank wins even when the winning live file is uncommitted or sourceRef names another branch. If an attention candidate has a valid RFC3339 `last_updated_at` later than a higher normal candidate, it wins; attention wins exact or missing-timestamp safety ties. For equal normal ranks, a later valid RFC3339 `last_updated_at` wins; otherwise retain the committed Oracle baseline. No other source participates.

On a repository catalog failure, surface the deterministic catalog error through the existing TUI error path and do not populate a partial release list or silently fall back to a different board. Retain the existing filesystem-only release-list fallback for non-Git test/initialisation directories where no Git HEAD is available.

Add integration-level TUI fixtures in real temporary Git repositories. One starts with no release board on HEAD, a valid `refs/heads/release-wt/<release>` board, a committed owner-track baseline, and a matching uncommitted current-checkout status at a higher lifecycle rank; drive the Model release-list entry point and its returned asynchronous command, then assert the release is listed, selected, and loaded with the selected source ref, tracks, and higher live state. Additional table cases must prove a lower live state cannot regress the baseline, a later attention state cannot be masked by an older success, and malformed or identity-mismatched live files cannot override it.
out_of_scope: All-ref enumeration, aggregate CLI JSON/text formatting, canonical skew handling, and mutation proof are S01-all-ref-board-catalog.
TUI redesign, new keyboard bindings, live-view/log-view/blocked-view ref routing, and changes to unrelated release-mode commands remain out of scope for #123.
Making the TUI fetch remote refs or mutate local Git state is forbidden; it consumes only S01's locally available catalog.

Changing CLI board state arbitration to read uncommitted working-tree files is out of scope. `sworn board` remains a committed-ref-only automation contract; this slice changes only the TUI display after it consumes C-01.

effort_complexity.rationale: The changed file surface is small and follows the catalog produced by S01, but the boundary is subtle: a ref-only plan must never be replaced by a stale filesystem board while the active-checkout live-status exception still works.

risks.description: The list and board view independently resolve the same release and can choose different source refs.
risks.mitigation: Persist sourceRef in the selected ReleaseInfo and thread it through the Enter command, BoardView, reload path, and async stale-result identity. The TUI must consume S01's catalog rather than call a separate resolver.

risks.description: A valid uncommitted current-checkout status is hidden merely because sourceRef points elsewhere, or an older/invalid live file regresses or masks the committed Oracle baseline.
risks.mitigation: Compare only identity-matching current-checkout and committed Oracle candidates with the specified lifecycle rank, timestamp, and attention-state tie rules. Cover higher-live, lower-live, later-attention, malformed, and identity-mismatch cases in TUI fixtures.

risks.description: A catalog failure leaves a stale or incomplete releases pane that looks successful to an operator.
risks.mitigation: Treat discovery errors as TUI errors, clear/avoid partial list population, and retain filesystem fallback only for an explicitly non-Git directory.

risks.description: Ref-aware loading blocks Bubble Tea's update loop or regresses keyboard navigation.
risks.mitigation: Keep Git reads inside the existing asynchronous load command and verify Enter returns a command before the board is populated, with existing j/k/up/down/Enter behaviour retained.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the TUI starts from a temporary Git repository whose checked-out HEAD contains no release board but `refs/heads/release-wt/ref-only-release` contains a valid board and `refs/heads/track/ref-only-release/T1-core` contains a newer `S01-alpha` status, THE SYSTEM SHALL list `ref-only-release` in the releases pane. WHEN the operator selects it and presses Enter, the returned asynchronous load command SHALL populate the board with the ref-only plan's tracks and committed Oracle baseline; the loaded board and selected ReleaseInfo SHALL identify `refs/heads/release-wt/ref-only-release` as sourceRef (C-01, N-03, N-04).

WHEN a catalog-selected `sourceRef` is either the active branch or a different branch, and its committed Oracle baseline for `S01-alpha` is `planned` while the current checkout has a readable, parseable, identity-matching uncommitted `status.json` for `S01-alpha` at `implemented`, THE SYSTEM SHALL show `implemented` without changing sourceRef or board-plan topology. WHEN the live candidate has a lower normal lifecycle rank than the baseline, it SHALL not regress the displayed state. WHEN a live `blocked`, `failed_verification`, or `deferred` attention candidate has a valid later `last_updated_at` than a higher normal baseline, or timestamps tie or are absent, it SHALL be shown rather than masked. WHEN the live file is absent, malformed, has an unknown state, or its release/slice/track identity differs, it SHALL not override the committed baseline. For equal normal ranks, a later valid RFC3339 `last_updated_at` SHALL win and otherwise the committed baseline SHALL win. The committed baseline SHALL resolve an owning track branch before the selected release source; arbitrary other refs and worktrees SHALL not be consulted (N-03, N-04).

WHEN the catalog resolver returns a missing, malformed, or identity-mismatched canonical-board error during TUI initialisation, THE SYSTEM SHALL expose that error through the existing TUI error message and SHALL not render a partial successful release list or substitute a lower-priority board. WHEN a temporary supplied root has no usable Git HEAD but contains filesystem `board.json`, `index.md`, and `status.json` records for `local-alpha` and `local-zeta`, THE SYSTEM SHALL use the non-Git fallback, set both ReleaseInfo sourceRef values to empty, list exactly `[local-alpha, local-zeta]` in bytewise lexical order, and load `local-alpha` into its declared `T1-core` board after Enter and its returned command complete (N-03, N-04).

WHEN the catalog supplies two release IDs in bytewise order, `alpha` then `zeta`, THE SYSTEM SHALL initialise the releases cursor at `alpha`; `j` and down SHALL move it exactly one position toward `zeta` without moving past the final entry, and `k` and up SHALL move it exactly one position toward `alpha` without moving before the first entry. WHEN Enter selects `zeta`, the same Update call SHALL set the Board release name to `zeta`, set `Loaded` false and `Loading` true, render `Loading…`, and return a non-nil asynchronous command; only delivery of that command's result for the same release ID and sourceRef SHALL set `Loaded` true and populate the board. A result with the same release ID but a different sourceRef SHALL leave the pending/selected board unchanged. `go test ./internal/tui ./internal/board ./cmd/sworn`, `go test ./...`, and `go vet ./...` SHALL exit 0, and `gofmt -l .` SHALL produce empty output (C-01, N-03, N-04).
